ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUN 17, 2026 · BUILT JUN 17 ~06:00 ET (PRE-RTH)

ES Trading Plan — Jun 17 ETH & RTH

Charts: TPO profile + 30-min (06:00 ET) · Prior RTH Jun 16 = distribution day, closed below value · Overnight repaired the weak close and balanced · Catalyst: first Warsh FOMC (decision ~2:00 PM ET) — consensus hold, dots/SEP/presser the wildcard
Last (pre-open) 7,596
Jun 15 RTH... → Jun 16 RTH: O 7,627.75 · H 7,648.75 · C 7,587.75 (below value)
ON: HIGH 7,612.75 · POC 7,604.00 · VAL 7,592.75 · LOW 7,577.75
yVAH 7,622.75 · yPOC 7,607.25 · yVAL 7,599.00
vs 20-EMA 7,527 +69 · above ALL MAs
ATH 7,694.75 +99 overhead · ATR 103
VIX 18.35 (sub-19, prior chart)
▲ What changed overnight (Jun 16 RTH close → Jun 17 pre-open) — the weak close got bought back Jun 16 RTH printed a distribution day: opened 7,627.75, tagged the 7,648.75 poor high and rejected, sold the rest of the session and closed 7,587.75 — below the value area (a weak, bearish-looking close). The expectation was downside follow-through. Instead, overnight did not continue lower: price based at 7,577.75 (just under the close), balanced, and built fresh value back up — ON value 7,592.75–7,607.75, POC 7,604.00, high 7,612.75 — rotating back into the lower half of yesterday's value (ON POC 7,604 sits between yVAL 7,599 and yPOC 7,607). Responsive buyers defended the weak close, so the immediate bearish edge is gone; the setup is now balanced/neutral into the Fed rather than breaking down. Price coils ~7,596 between ON VAL 7,592.75 and ON POC 7,604, still capped by yVAH 7,622.75 and the 7,648.75 poor high. The day's direction now hinges on the 2pm Warsh decision: coil before, expansion after.

01Top-Down — Overnight Recap

The overnight (Jun 16 18:00 → Jun 17 06:00 ET) is essentially complete. Its job was to digest the weak Jun 16 close — and it did so constructively, basing at 7,577.75 and rebuilding value up to a 7,604 POC.

a) Daily — context (carried; no fresh Daily attached this round)

MAValueDistStatus
20-EMA7,527.00+69RISING — first dynamic support; the line that defines the swing
50-EMA7,388.75+207Rising
200-EMA7,029.50+566Rising — heavy-volume shelf well below

Daily read: Still a full bull stack (price above 20>50>100>200>300). Jun 16's distribution candle pulled back from the 7,648.75 high but did not break structure; only the ATH 7,694.75 is overhead. Slow-moving MA values carried from the prior Daily chart (not re-attached this round).

b) 30-Min — overnight flow

SessionHighLowClose≈Character
Jun 16 RTH7,648.75~7,5787,587.75Distribution: tag poor high, reject, close below value
Jun 16→17 ON7,612.757,577.75~7,596Repair/balance: based under the close, rebuilt value up to POC 7,604

30-min read: The overnight is a responsive-buy balance. It defended 7,577.75, pushed back to 7,612.75 (back above yVAL/yPOC briefly), and settled around 7,596 with POC 7,604. No reclaim of yVAH 7,622.75 — so balanced, not bullishly resolved.

c) Profile — overnight TPO

MetricValueRead
ON HIGH / LOW7,612.75 / 7,577.7535-pt overnight range — contained
ON VAH / POC / VAL7,607.75 / 7,604.00 / 7,592.75Value rebuilt UP into the lower half of yesterday's value
vs yesterdayON POC 7,604 between yVAL 7,599 & yPOC 7,607Overlap = acceptance back inside value (repair)

Profile read: Overnight value overlaps the bottom third of Jun 16's value — the market accepted prices back inside the range it rejected at the close. That is a repair, and it neutralizes the weak-close downside tell heading into RTH.

d) VIX

No VIX chart attached this round. Last reading VX1! 18.35 (sub-19, downtrending) — a risk-on backdrop. Treat as context only; watch for an intraday pop > 20–21 on a hawkish Fed as the risk-off tell.

RankPathDirProb

Base case (pre-RTH)Balance inside overnight value into the open. The overnight repair means RTH most likely opens in value (~7,592–7,612) and waits for the Fed — see the RTH plan.

06Decision Map — Pre-RTH

ON balance ~7,596 (value 7,592–7,612 · POC 7,604)
│
├─► holds 7,592 → drift to open in value → RTH R1
├─► reclaims 7,607/7,612 → RTH R2 repair lean
└─► loses 7,587 → test ON LOW 7,577.75

      └─ fails → RTH R3 → 7,565 fib

07Bias Flips — Overnight

FromToTriggerAction
BalanceFirmAccept > 7,607Lean R2 into the open
BalanceSoftLose 7,587.75Watch 7,577.75 for the dip-buy / breakdown decision

08Playbook — Pre-RTH (light)

Pre-Fed = small or flatThe overnight is a coil and the Fed is hours away. Best practice into the open: keep size light, fade the overnight edges (buy 7,592 / sell 7,612) only as scalps, and save the real risk for the RTH plan and the post-2pm move.

09Warnings — Overnight

✓ Overnight repaired the weak closeResponsive buyers defended 7,577.75 and rebuilt value to 7,604 — the bearish close tell is neutralized. Don't carry yesterday's bear bias blindly into RTH.
⚠ Still cappedNo reclaim of yVAH 7,622.75 or the 7,648.75 poor high. Balanced, not bullish — and the Fed can break it either way.

10Transition — Overnight → RTH open

If at the open price is…RTH implication
> 7,612Above overnight value → bull repair lean (R2)
7,592–7,612In value → balance/coil, Fed decides (R1) — most likely
7,577–7,592Lower edge → dip-buy watch; 7,577 must hold
< 7,577Below ON low → breakdown risk (R3) toward 7,565

11Summary — Overnight

ItemCall
Overnight resultBalance / repair — based 7,577.75, rebuilt value to POC 7,604
Effect on biasNeutralized the weak Jun 16 close; RTH enters balanced, not bearish
Open leanMost likely opens in value 7,592–7,612 → see RTH plan
Key level7,577.75 (ON low) holds = constructive · loses = 7,565 gap-fill path

01Top-Down Analysis — RTH (Wed Jun 17, Fed-day)

Reference: pre-open ~7,596, inside overnight value (7,592–7,612), below yVAH 7,622.75 and the 7,648.75 poor high. Jun 16 closed weak but the overnight bought it back — so RTH enters balanced into the 2pm Warsh decision, with the daily uptrend still intact beneath.

a) Daily — MA stack & drawn levels (carried; no fresh Daily this round)

MAValueDistanceStatus
20-EMA7,527.00+69RISING — only dynamic support that matters intraday; the swing line
50-EMA7,388.75+207Rising — not in play barring a multi-day reversal
100-EMA7,229.00+367Rising
200-EMA7,029.50+566Rising — heavy-volume shelf
300-EMA6,864.25+732Rising
Drawn (Daily)PriceDistNote
ATH / swing high7,694.75+99The only overhead structure — upside magnet
fib extend7,621.75+26Clusters with yVAH 7,622.75 — the balance top
fib extend7,565.00−31Gate to the gap-fill path

Daily narrative: Full bull MA stack (20>50>100>200>300, all rising), price above all of them, +69 over the 20-EMA. Jun 16's distribution candle was a pullback from the 7,648.75 high, not a structural break. The only overhead reference is the ATH 7,694.75; the 20-EMA 7,527 is the line that turns an intraday pullback into a swing question — far below, so most RTH outcomes are bullish-to-neutral. (Daily values carried from the prior session's chart; no fresh Daily attached this round.)

b) 30-Min — session flow & drawn levels

30-min narrative: The tape is a failed-breakout (Jun 16) → overnight repair sequence. Overhead the caps stack tight: 7,604 (ON POC) → 7,607–7,608 (yPOC/ON VAH) → 7,612.75 (ON HIGH) → 7,621.75–7,622.75 (yVAH/fib) → 7,648.75 (poor high) — a resistance ladder into the highs. Below, support is well-defined and value-backed: 7,592.75 (ON VAL) → 7,587.75 (y.close) → 7,577.75 (ON low) → 7,565 (fib) → 7,527 (20-EMA). Current ~7,596 sits at the lower-middle of overnight value, between ON VAL and ON POC.

c) Market Profile — Jun 16 RTH + overnight

MetricValueRead
Jun 16 O / H / C7,627.75 / 7,648.75 / 7,587.75Open upper third, tag poor high, close below value = distribution
Jun 16 VAH / POC / VAL7,622.75 / 7,607.25 / 7,599.00Value high; close fell out the bottom of it
ON HIGH / LOW7,612.75 / 7,577.7535-pt overnight range — contained
ON VAH / POC / VAL7,607.75 / 7,604.00 / 7,592.75Value rebuilt UP into the lower half of Jun 16 value
Poor / unfinished high7,648.75Rejected once — magnet AND cap; repair needs a HELD break
Shape (Jun 16)b-variant / distribution (open high, close low)Late-session selling; the soft tell that overnight then repaired
STRUCTURE MAP (pre-RTH) 7,694.75 ── ATH ◄ magnet above 7,648.75 ── JUN 16 HIGH (poor / unfinished) 7,632.25 ── 30m line 7,627.75 ── y.open 7,622.75 ── yVAH / 7,621.75 fib ◄ balance top / gate 7,612.75 ── ON HIGH 7,607.75 ── ON VAH / 7,607.25 yPOC 7,605.50 ── 30m line 7,604.00 ── ON POC ◄ value magnet 7,599.00 ── yVAL 7,596.00 ◄◄ YOU ARE HERE (pre-open) 7,592.75 ── ON VAL 7,587.75 ── y.close (weak close / Jun 14 POC area) 7,578.25 ── 30m line / 7,577.75 ON LOW 7,565.00 ── fib extend ░ gap-fill gate ░ 7,550.50 ── 30m line 7,527.50 / 7,527.00 ── 30m line + daily 20-EMA

Profile narrative: Two stacked frames — Jun 16's value (7,599–7,622.75) sitting on the overnight's value (7,592.75–7,607.75). The key event is that overnight built acceptance back inside the lower half of yesterday's range (ON POC 7,604 between yVAL and yPOC). Translation: the weak Jun 16 close was responsively bought overnight, so RTH does not inherit a clean downside continuation. The market is balanced: bulls need to reclaim and hold 7,607–7,608 to press the 7,612 → 7,622 → 7,648 ladder; bears need to lose 7,592.75 → 7,587.75 and accept below to open the 7,577 → 7,565 path. The 2pm Fed is the catalyst that resolves the coil.

d) VIX + macro context

No VIX chart attached this round; last reading VX1! 18.35 (sub-19, downtrend) — a risk-on, melt backdrop. Macro: the dominant catalyst is the first Warsh FOMC (decision ~2:00 PM ET) — consensus is a hold; the move comes from the dot plot, SEP, and presser tone. Secondary: a rotation out of tech (NQ heavy, semis weak) with cyclicals firmer — a narrowing tape that makes ES upside lower-quality if NQ keeps lagging; oil < $80 supporting bonds. A brief MOC note from yesterday's close (side not specified): S&P ~$2.20B / NQ ~$1.36B / Dow ~$577M — moderate; if it was sell-side it fits the weak Jun 16 close, but the overnight already bought it back. Watch a VIX pop > 20–21 intraday as the risk-off tell.

DimensionReadingImplication for RTH

Settlement locationJun 16 closed 7,587.75 below value; ON rebuilt to POC 7,604Weak close bought back overnight = balanced entry
Profile shapeb/distribution (Jun 16) → balance (overnight)Two-sided; resistance ladder above, value-backed support below

04What-If Scenarios — RTH (open-location + Fed)

R1 · Pre-Fed Balance / In-Value Rotation — 7,592–7,622NEUTRAL★★★★ 30%
Zone / contextOpens inside overnight value (7,592–7,612), two-sided around ON POC 7,604 / yPOC 7,607 ahead of 2pm.
NarrativeRotation between ON VAL 7,592 and the yVAH 7,622 cap; buy 7,592–7,599 dips, fade 7,612–7,622. The base-case AM until the Fed breaks it.
SetupLong 7,594–7,597 (mid-value) · Stop 7,586 (under y.close) · T1 7,604 (+8) · T2 7,612 (+16) · T3 7,622 (+26) · R:R 0.9→3.0. Scalp short 7,620–7,622 · Stop 7,629 · T 7,608/7,604.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: rejection wicks at 7,612–7,622 + bids at 7,592. Invalid: clean break either edge → R2 / R3.
R2 · Value Reclaim → Poor-High Repair — 7,608 → 7,648BULL★★★ 22%
Zone / contextAccepts above 7,607–7,608 (yPOC/ON VAH), clears 7,612.75 (ON HIGH).
NarrativeThe overnight repair extends: grind to 7,621.75–7,622.75 (yVAH/fib), then the 7,648.75 poor high. A held break > 7,648.75 puts the ATH 7,694 in play. The weak close becomes a pre-Fed shakeout.
SetupLong 7,608–7,610 on the reclaim/hold · Stop 7,599 (back under yVAL) · T1 7,612.75 (+4) · T2 7,622 (+13) · T3 7,648 (+39) · R:R 0.4→4.3; runner only on a held break > 7,648.75.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: NQ firms / leads; 2× 30-min closes hold > 7,608. Invalid: rejection at 7,622 + back < 7,604 → R1.
R3 · Below-Value Breakdown — 7,592 → 7,577 → 7,565BEAR — real short★★★ 20%
Zone / contextLoses ON VAL 7,592.75 and y.close 7,587.75, accepts below.
NarrativeThe overnight repair fails — sellers reclaim control: 7,587.75 → ON LOW 7,577.75 → 7,565 fib. A directional short, not a dip-buy locator.
SetupShort 7,588–7,591 on a failed retest of 7,592/7,599 (or short the 7,577 break) · Stop 7,601 (back above yVAL) · T1 7,577.75 (−12) · T2 7,565 (−25) · T3 7,550 (−40) · R:R 1.0→3.5.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: acceptance < 7,587.75 (30-min close). Invalid: reclaim > 7,599 → flat / flip to R1.
R4 · Fed-Flush Dip-Buy (trend defense) — 7,527BULL★★ 13%
Zone / contextA hawkish 2pm knee-jerk flushes through 7,565 to the 20-EMA 7,527.
NarrativeBuyers defend the daily trend at the 20-EMA — the higher-TF edge. This is the deep, value-supported long, taken only after the print with a response/absorption signal.
SetupLong 7,527–7,531 on a response · Stop 7,508 (size down — wide) · T1 7,550 (+20) · T2 7,565 (+35) · T3 7,578 (+48) · R:R ~1.0→2.3.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: bid/absorption at 7,527 post-Fed. Invalid: daily close < 7,527 → R5, stand down.
R5 · Hawkish Breakdown — < 7,527BEAR TAIL★ 10%
Zone / contextHawkish dots/SEP; VIX pops > 21; accept below the 20-EMA.
NarrativeThe swing-top scenario activates: 7,527 → 7,508 → 7,491. This is where the daily uptrend is genuinely questioned.
SetupNo fresh shorts into the hole; short only on a failed retest of 7,527 from below · or pre-stage responsive longs lower. R:R situational.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX > 21, 30-min accept < 7,527. Invalid: reclaim > 7,550 → back to range.
R6 · Dovish Melt-Up → ATH — > 7,648BULL OUTLIER★ 5%
Zone / contextDovish Fed surprise; held break of the 7,648.75 poor high.
NarrativeOpen-air to the ATH 7,694.75 same session; measured-move risk above if the ATH gives way.
SetupDon't chase — buy the first pullback holding 7,648.75 from above · Stop 7,640 · T1 7,673.50 · T2 7,694 · R:R high. First ATH tag usually rejects — trim hard.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: volume expansion + VIX < 18. Invalid: back under 7,640 → fade.

05Probability Matrix — RTH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1R1 Pre-Fed balance / in-value rotation 7,592–7,622Neutral30%★★★★
2R2 Value reclaim → poor-high repair → 7,648Bull22%★★★
3R3 Below-value breakdown → 7,577/7,565Bear20%★★★
4R4 Fed-flush dip-buy at 20-EMA 7,527Bull13%★★
5R5 Hawkish breakdown < 7,527Bear tail10%★
6R6 Dovish melt-up → ATH 7,694Bull outlier5%★
CombinedBull 40%Bear 30%Neutral 30%
Base case (RTH)A pre-Fed two-sided coil between ~7,592 and 7,622 around ON POC 7,604 / yPOC 7,607, then the 2pm Warsh decision sets direction. Reasoning: the overnight repair removed the immediate bearish edge (responsive buying defended the weak close), the daily trend is up, and price is mid-value — so the lean is neutral-to-constructive, not bearish. Bulls need to reclaim & hold 7,607–7,608 to press 7,612 → 7,622 → 7,648; bears need acceptance below 7,587.75 to open 7,577 → 7,565. Trade the second move after 2pm, not the knee-jerk.

06Decision Map — RTH

RTH OPEN ~7,596 (ON value 7,592–7,612 · POC 7,604 · yVAH 7,622 cap · poor high 7,648 · ATH 7,694)
│
├─► ACCEPTS > 7,608 (reclaims yPOC / ON VAH)
│     ├─ clears 7,612.75 ON HIGH → tag yVAH 7,621.75–7,622.75
│     │     ├─ rejected → fade back to 7,604 / 7,599
│     │     └─ accepts > 7,622.75 → R2/R6 → 7,648 poor high → fib 7,694 / ATH
│     └─ stalls under 7,612 → rotate in value 7,592–7,612
│
├─► OPENS 7,592–7,608 (inside value — most likely)
│     ├─ holds ON POC 7,604 → R1 balance → Fed breaks the tie
│     └─ loses 7,592.75 ON VAL → test y.close 7,587.75
│            ├─ holds 7,587 → responsive bid → back to value
│            └─ closes < 7,587.75 → R3 → ON LOW 7,577.75
│                  ├─ 7,577 bought → 7,592 / 7,604
│                  └─ < 7,577 → 7,565 fib → 7,550 → 20-EMA 7,527
│
└─► OPENS < 7,577 (gap-down headline)
      → R3/R4 → 7,565 → 7,550 → buy the 20-EMA 7,527 shelf, not the hole
LevelHoldsBreaks
7,694.75 (ATH)Day-one cap — fadeOpen air → measured move
7,648.75 (poor high)Resistance — rotateTrend leg → 7,673 → ATH
7,621.75–7,622.75 (yVAH/fib)Balance top — fade to valuePress to 7,648 (R2)
7,604–7,608 (value)Reclaim = bull control; buy dipsSlip to ON VAL / yVAL
7,592.75 (ON VAL)Overnight floor — dip heldTest y.close 7,587.75
7,587.75 (y.close)Bull intact — responsive bidR3 → ON low 7,577.75
7,577.75 (ON low)Repair holds — major dip-buyGap-fill: 7,565 → 7,550 → 20-EMA 7,527
7,527 (20-EMA)Trend defense — buy the shelfSwing question → 7,508/7,491 (R5)

07Bias Flip Conditions — RTH

Starting biasFlip toTriggerAction
Neutral (default)BullAccept > 7,608 (2× 30-min)Long; target 7,612 → 7,622 → 7,648; trail under 7,604
BullBreakout bullHeld break > 7,648.75Add; target fib 7,694 / ATH; trail under 7,632
NeutralBear30-min close < 7,587.75R3 short → 7,577.75 / 7,565; no longs above value
BearBear (deeper)Accept < 7,527 + VIX > 21Gap-fill / swing-top; pre-stage longs lower, no chasing shorts
BearRepairReclaim > 7,604Back to balance / R2 lean toward 7,622

08Tactical Playbook — RTH (Top 3)

#1
In-Value Dip-Buy / Reclaim (R1→R2) — base-case long
LONGup to 4.3R▶
Entry
Long 7,594–7,597 (mid-value) on a hold, or 7,608–7,610 on a reclaim of yPOC/ON VAH
Stop
7,586 (under y.close) for the dip · 7,599 for the reclaim · risk 8–11 pts
Targets
T1 7,604 (+8)T2 7,612 (+16)T3 7,622 (+26) then 7,648 on a held break
Scaling
½ at ON POC 7,604 · ¼ at ON HIGH 7,612 · runner to yVAH 7,622; add-back on a held break > 7,622
R:R
~1.0R / 2.0R / 3.0R (→4.3R into 7,648)
Kill switch
30-min close < 7,587.75 → flat, wait for the 7,577.75 shelf (R3)
#2
Below-Value Breakdown (R3) — the real short
SHORTup to 3.5R▶
Entry
Short 7,588–7,591 on a failed retest of 7,592/7,599 from below (or short the 7,577 break-retest)
Stop
7,601 (back above yVAL) · risk 10–13 pts
Targets
T1 7,577.75 (−12)T2 7,565 (−25)T3 7,550 (−40)
Scaling
½ at ON low 7,577.75 · ¼ at fib 7,565 · cover the rest at 7,550 (don't press into the 7,527 dip-buy zone)
R:R
1.0R / 2.1R / 3.5R
Kill switch
Reclaim > 7,599 (30-min close) → flat, flip to #1
#3
Fed-Flush Trend-Defense (R4) — deep dip long
LONGup to 2.3R▶
Entry
Long 7,527–7,531 (20-EMA) on a post-FOMC flush with a response/absorption signal
Stop
7,508 · risk 19–23 pts — size down hard
Targets
T1 7,550 (+20)T2 7,565 (+35)T3 7,578 (+48)
Scaling
⅓ at T1 · ⅓ at 7,565 · runner to 7,578; the value-supported, highest-quality location of the three
R:R
~1.0R / 1.7R / 2.3R
Kill switch
Daily close < 7,527 → exit; swing-top regime (R5), stand down
Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 103 on a Fed day: run ~½ normal size and don't carry a fresh position through 2pm — a single FOMC candle can cover the whole expected day's range. Let the print clear, then engage the second move. Full-to-70% size only on the value-supported dip-buys (#1, #3); the breakout add (> 7,622 / > 7,648) is ¼-size until a confirmed close.

09Structural Warnings — RTH

✓ Overnight repaired the weak close — don't carry a stale bear biasJun 16 closed below value (bearish), but the overnight responsively bought it back and rebuilt value to POC 7,604. RTH enters balanced, not breaking down. The first instinct to "short the weak close" is already late.
⚠ Resistance ladder 7,604 → 7,612 → 7,622 → 7,648Four caps stacked into the highs. The day must grind through each to reach the poor high. Don't buy mid-ladder; buy the value dips (7,592–7,599) and let acceptance prove itself before chasing 7,622/7,648.
⚠ Poor / unfinished high 7,648.75Rejected once. It's a magnet that can pull price up (don't get trapped short under it) but the first re-tag usually rejects (don't get trapped long into it). Trade the held break, not the touch.
⚠ 2pm Fed = vol expansionThe knee-jerk is frequently faded. Stand aside through the initial spike; trade the second leg. Cut size into the print. ATR 103 means one candle can be the whole day.
⚠ Tech rotation / narrowing bidNQ/semis heavy while cyclicals firm = lower-quality breadth. If ES presses up but NQ lags again, distrust the move. NQ reclaiming leadership re-validates R2.
ℹ 7,527 (20-EMA) is the swing lineAbove it, the daily uptrend is intact and deep dips are buys (R4). A daily close below 7,527 would be the first real character change since the rally — the swing-bear trigger (R5), not intraday wobble.

10Session Transition — RTH open outcomes

If at the open price is…ImplicationLikely day type
> 7,622Above the balance top / yVAHBreakout-up attempt → 7,648 test (beware first-tag rejection)
7,608–7,622Reclaimed value, gate pendingBull-lean rotation; buy dips, yVAH the pivot (R2)
7,592–7,608 (in value)Balanced around ON POC / yPOCTwo-sided rotation; Fed breaks the tie (R1) — most likely
7,587–7,592Lower value edgeDip-buy watch; 7,587.75 / 7,577.75 must hold
7,565–7,587Below the close / ON low brokenDown-trend-day risk; sell rallies to 7,592 (R3)
< 7,565Gap-fill openFlush to 20-EMA 7,527, then dip-buy response (R4)

11Final Summary — RTH Wed Jun 17

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL, pausing — full bull MA stack rising, VIX sub-19; offset by tech rotation + Fed event risk
Intermediate biasBULL, digesting — Jun 16 distribution pulled back from 7,648, overnight repaired it; only the ATH 7,694 overhead
Intraday biasNEUTRAL → BULL-lean into the Fed — overnight repair removed the bear edge; buy value dips, the Fed decides
Base casePre-Fed balance 7,592–7,622 around ON POC 7,604 → 2pm sets direction; reclaim 7,608 → 7,622/7,648, lose 7,587 → 7,577/7,565
Primary tradeLong 7,594–7,597 (mid-value) / 7,608–7,610 reclaim, stop 7,586/7,599 → 7,604/7,612/7,622 (R1→R2)
Best-location alternateLong 7,527–7,531 at the 20-EMA on a Fed flush, stop 7,508 → 7,550/7,565/7,578 (R4)
Real shortShort 7,588–7,591 on a failed retest after acceptance < 7,587.75, stop 7,601 → 7,577/7,565/7,550 (R3)
Key bull level7,607–7,608 (yPOC/ON VAH) reclaim → 7,612/7,622; held break > 7,648.75 → ATH 7,694
Key bear level7,587.75 (y.close) — acceptance below → 7,577.75 / 7,565; 7,527 = the swing line
Biggest riskFed knee-jerk whipsaw (both ways); the resistance ladder rejects the first push; tech rotation hollowing the bid
Day-type expectationPre-Fed balance (~30%) · post-Fed trend-up/repair (~40% incl. melt) · below-value breakdown (~20%) · gap-fill/flush (~10%)
ES Plan for Jun 17, 2026 (ETH + RTH) · built Jun 17 ~06:00 ET (pre-RTH) from TPO + 30-min charts · Pre-open ~7,596 · Jun 16 RTH: O 7,627.75 / H 7,648.75 / C 7,587.75 (below value) · Overnight: HIGH 7,612.75 / POC 7,604.00 / VAL 7,592.75 / LOW 7,577.75 · yVAH 7,622.75 · yPOC 7,607.25 · yVAL 7,599.00 · ATH 7,694.75 · ATR 103 · 20-EMA 7,527 (carried) · VIX 18.35 (carried; no VIX chart this round) · Catalyst: first Warsh FOMC ~2pm ET · Not financial advice. Re-anchor R/S to the actual cash open.
